mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a backup and restore operator for Kubernetes that allows you to back up your cluster resources and persistent volumes. It is useful for disaster recovery, migration, and replication of clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration of cloud storage and potential complexity in managing backups and restores.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This repository contains the Velero Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Velero CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Velero to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-crds.yaml

```

> This command fetches and installs the CRDs for Velero version 1.18.0.

steps.title: Install Velero using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Velero in the specified namespace with the necessary configuration.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=us-west-2

```

> Replace 'my-velero-bucket' with your actual S3 bucket name.
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=us-west-2

steps.title: Create a Backup Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a backup resource to ensure Velero is functioning correctly.

```bash

velero backup create test-backup --include-namespaces default

```

> This command creates a backup of the default namespace.

steps.title: Verify the Backup
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the backup to confirm it was created successfully.

```bash

velero backup get

```

> This command lists all backups and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This step removes Velero and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all backups and data associated with Velero.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This command uninstalls Velero from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration and data. This step will upgrade Velero to the latest version.

```bash

velero backup get

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

velero version

```

> Check existing backups before proceeding.
> This command upgrades Velero to version 1.18.0.
> Verify the upgrade by checking the Velero version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup: the specified bucket does not exist'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The S3 bucket specified in the configuration does not exist or is incorrectly named.

**Fix:**
Ensure the S3 bucket exists and the name is correctly specified in the Helm install command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server (the server could not find the requested resource)'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRDs were not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-crds.yaml.

troubleshooting.title: Backup status shows 'InProgress' indefinitely
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to insufficient permissions or issues with the storage backend.

**Fix:**
Check the permissions for the S3 bucket and ensure Velero has access to it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'VolumeSnapshotLocation not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified VolumeSnapshotLocation in the configuration does not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the VolumeSnapshotLocation is created and correctly referenced in the Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for AWS EBS CSI driver, improved error handling for backup and restore operations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17 or earlier, ensure to update your backup storage location configuration to match the new requirements.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of backups with their statuses when running 'velero backup get'. A successful backup will show 'Completed' status.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources and tools installed.
